novel_features.name: This-week schedule
novel_features.description: Every game and practice for the current week across all your players' teams, in one view.
novel_features.rationale: The dashboard shows one team's calendar at a time; this merges all your players' teams with week-boundary date math.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when asked 'what's on this week' for a family with kids on different teams.
novel_features.group: Schedule, the way a parent asks

novel_features.name: Next event
novel_features.description: The single next upcoming game or practice across all your players' teams: what, when, where, home/away, opponent.
novel_features.rationale: A cross-team min(start) answer no single dashboard screen gives you.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this for 'when is my kid's next game' or a one-line reminder.
novel_features.group: Schedule, the way a parent asks

novel_features.name: Merged family agenda
novel_features.description: Both kids' separate team schedules merged into one chronological agenda over an N-day window.
novel_features.rationale: Joins multiple children's team schedules into one list — impossible in the per-team SPA.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to plan the family's next two weeks in a single sorted list.
novel_features.group: Schedule, the way a parent asks

novel_features.name: Conflict finder
novel_features.description: Flags time overlaps and impossible tight time-plus-location gaps between events across all your players.
novel_features.rationale: A pairwise self-join over events that no single calendar view performs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to catch a double-booked Saturday before Saturday morning.
novel_features.group: Schedule, the way a parent asks

novel_features.name: Away-game drive list
novel_features.description: Away games only, location-first, with field, opponent, and date — built for the carpool group chat.
novel_features.rationale: Filters the home/away content pattern and resolves locations into a drive list the dashboard never assembles.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when planning away-game driving or posting a travel list.
novel_features.group: Schedule, the way a parent asks

novel_features.name: iCal export
novel_features.description: Emit the merged schedule as an RFC-5545 .ics file for phone-calendar subscription.
novel_features.rationale: No remote endpoint emits calendar files; this synthesizes one from fetched events.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to get soccer events into Apple/Google Calendar.
novel_features.group: Schedule, the way a parent asks

novel_features.name: Changed since last check
novel_features.description: What schedule items were added, moved, or cancelled since the last time you ran it.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs a stored prior snapshot against a fresh fetch; the dashboard has no 'what changed' view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to catch a moved practice or a newly-added game.
novel_features.group: Schedule, the way a parent asks

novel_features.name: Outstanding balance roll-up
novel_features.description: Total money owed across all your players: registration balances plus overdue invoices, with one number.
novel_features.rationale: Joins registration balances with overdue invoices into a cross-player total that exists in neither API call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this for 'what do we still owe the club' across multiple kids.

novel_features.name: Registration deadline radar
novel_features.description: Open registration programs sorted by how soon they close, flagging ones closing within N days.
novel_features.rationale: Adds close-date math and a threshold flag over the raw open-programs list.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to avoid missing a registration window.

narrative.headline: Your youth-soccer club's schedule, teams, and dues from the terminal — with merged multi-kid agendas, conflict detection, and iCal export no dashboard gives you.
narrative.value_prop: Sprocket Sports CLI wraps your club's Sprocket dashboard API so you can answer the questions a parent actually asks: 'when is the next game' (next), 'what's on this week' (week), 'plan the next two weeks for both kids' (agenda), 'any double-bookings' (conflicts), and 'what do we owe' (owed). It merges every one of your players' teams into a single view the per-team web dashboard can't produce, and exports everything to your phone calendar with ical.
narrative.auth_narrative: Sprocket Sports uses OAuth2/OIDC (Duende IdentityServer at login.sprocketsports.com) and the API takes a Bearer access token. This CLI authenticates with that token: set SPROCKET_TOKEN to the access_token from your logged-in dashboard session. To find it: open your club dashboard in Chrome, open DevTools, go to Application > Session Storage, find the 'oidc.user:...sprocket-sports' entry, and copy its access_token value. Tokens are short-lived (about an hour), so re-copy when commands start returning 401.

quickstart.comment: check that your token works and the API is reachable
your next game or practice across all your kids' teams
the next two weeks as one family agenda
away games with fields for the carpool chat

troubleshoots.fix: Your SPROCKET_TOKEN expired (tokens last about an hour). Re-copy the access_token from your dashboard session — see Authentication.

troubleshoots.symptom: 'Start date is out of valid range'
troubleshoots.fix: The calendar API caps a query at ~31 days. Use a smaller --days/--weeks value; next/week/agenda already stay inside the limit.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty schedule even though events exist
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm the token is for the right club tenant and that you have players assigned to teams: run 'sprocket teams mine'.
The base URL defaults to the Jacksonville FC tenant. Set the base URL in the config file to your own club's <club>.sprocketsports.com host.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or script needs a Sprocket Sports club's schedule, rosters, programs, registrations, or dues — especially merged across multiple children and teams, or as date-math (next game, this week, conflicts, away games) that the per-team web dashboard cannot answer in one view. It is ideal for reminders, carpool and away-game planning, iCal subscriptions, and 'what do we owe' roll-ups.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for league scores or standings — the parent dashboard API does not expose them (they are league-admin level).
Do not use it for coach or club announcements and messaging — that surface is not available here.
Do not use it to register, pay, RSVP, or otherwise change anything — v1 is strictly read-only.
Do not use it for a different club without first pointing the base URL at that club's tenant.

recipes.title: Next game as a one-line reminder
recipes.explanation: Narrow the deeply-nested event to just the fields a reminder needs; pipe to a text/cron job.

recipes.title: Two-week family agenda
recipes.explanation: Merge both kids' team schedules into one sorted list for the next two weeks.

recipes.title: Away-game drive list for the group chat
recipes.explanation: Just the away games, location-first, ready to paste to carpool parents.

recipes.title: What do we owe
recipes.explanation: One combined outstanding total across registrations and overdue invoices for all players.

recipes.title: Subscribe in your phone calendar
recipes.explanation: Write 60 days of merged events to an .ics file you can import or host for subscription.

trigger_phrases: when is my kid's next game
do we have any schedule conflicts
what do we owe the soccer club
